---
title: "kkookk"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
Gamma_values <- c(0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40)
colors <- c("blue", "red", "green", "purple")

E_Light <- 300
E <- seq(0, 1000, length.out = 300)

# First curve
gamma_new <- Gamma_values[1] * (1 - exp(-E / E_Light))

plot(E, gamma_new,
     type = "l",
     lwd = 2,
     col = colors[1],
     ylim = c(0, max(Gamma_values)),
     xlab = "Radiation",
     ylab = expression(gamma),
     main = expression("Effect of different " ~ gamma[max]))

# Remaining curves
for (i in 2:length(Gamma_values)) {
  
  gamma_new <- Gamma_values[i] * (1 - exp(-E / E_Light))
  
  lines(E, gamma_new,
        lwd = 2,
        col = colors[i])
}

legend("bottomright",
       legend = paste(expression(gamma[max]), "=", Gamma_values),
       col = colors,
       lwd = 2)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
# parameter values
delta_v <- 0.125
v_min <- 2^(-5)
m <- 57
i <- 1:m
v_i <- v_min * 2^((i - 1) * delta_v)

colors <- c("blue", "red", "green", "purple")


# 1. changing delta_max
a <- 4
b <- 10
delta_values <- c(0.02, 0.04, 0.06, 0.08)

plot(v_i,
     delta_values[1] * ((a*v_i)^b)/(1 + (a*v_i)^b),
     type = "l",
     col = colors[1],
     lwd = 2,
     ylim = c(0, max(delta_values)),
     xlab = "Cell volume",
     ylab = "delta_i",
     main = "Effect of delta_max")

for(j in 2:length(delta_values)) {
  delta_t <- delta_values[j] * ((a*v_i)^b)/(1 + (a*v_i)^b)
  lines(v_i, delta_t, col = colors[j], lwd = 2)
}

legend("bottomright",
       legend = paste("delta_max =", delta_values),
       col = colors,
       lwd = 2)


# 2. changing a
delta_max <- 0.06
b <- 10
a_values <- c(1, 2, 4, 8)

plot(v_i,
     delta_max * ((a_values[1]*v_i)^b)/(1 + (a_values[1]*v_i)^b),
     type = "l",
     col = colors[1],
     lwd = 2,
     ylim = c(0, delta_max),
     xlab = "Cell volume",
     ylab = "delta_i",
     main = "Effect of a")

for(j in 2:length(a_values)) {
  delta_t <- delta_max * ((a_values[j]*v_i)^b)/(1 + (a_values[j]*v_i)^b)
  lines(v_i, delta_t, col = colors[j], lwd = 2)
}

legend("bottomright",
       legend = paste("a =", a_values),
       col = colors,
       lwd = 2)


# 3. changing b
delta_max <- 0.06
a <- 4
b_values <- c(2, 5, 10, 20)

plot(v_i,
     delta_max * ((a*v_i)^b_values[1])/(1 + (a*v_i)^b_values[1]),
     type = "l",
     col = colors[1],
     lwd = 2,
     ylim = c(0, delta_max),
     xlab = "Cell volume",
     ylab = "delta_i",
     main = "Effect of b")

for(j in 2:length(b_values)) {
  delta_t <- delta_max * ((a*v_i)^b_values[j])/(1 + (a*v_i)^b_values[j])
  lines(v_i, delta_t, col = colors[j], lwd = 2)
}

legend("bottomright",
       legend = paste("b =", b_values),
       col = colors,
       lwd = 2)
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
